sanskrit: प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः। भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात्॥
transliteration: prakṛtiṃ svām avaṣṭabhya visṛjāmi punaḥ punaḥ | bhūtagrāmam imaṃ kṛtsnam avaśaṃ prakṛter vaśāt ||
literal_meaning: Taking hold of My own material nature, again and again I send forth this entire host of beings; they are powerless, being under the sway of prakṛti.
contextual_meaning: The Self repeatedly projects the inner universe of thoughts, emotions, and roles, yet none of these mental contents are ultimately under the ego’s control; they arise and dissolve according to deeper psychic laws.
psychological_state: Healthy dissociation from over-identification with compulsive mental patterns; recognition of the observing Self.
trigger_scenario: Lying awake while the mind replays tomorrow’s to-do list on loop—realizing the thoughts are not ‘me’ and cannot be forcibly stopped.
anchor_text: Watch the mind’s movie without claiming to direct the show.
